conditions: Elevated blood pressure (stress-related, not formally diagnosed as hypertension)
medications: Hydroxyzine 25 mg as needed for acute anxiety
Melatonin 3 mg at bedtime as needed
health.mental_health: Anxiety and depressive symptoms have intensified during the divorce and custody dispute; she has panic-like episodes before court-related meetings and reports sleep disruption and rumination. She is in weekly therapy and is cautious about anything that could be misconstrued in custody proceedings.
recent_visits: Primary care visit (6 weeks ago) for fatigue, sleep issues, and thyroid lab review
Endocrinology follow-up (3 months ago) to adjust levothyroxine dosage
Therapy session (weekly; most recent 5 days ago) focused on coping strategies for custody stress
Urgent care visit (2 months ago) for palpitations/anxiety; EKG normal

finance.debt: Car loan (~$9k remaining) and attorney retainer/ongoing legal fees for divorce and custody; she is also carrying a balance on one credit card due to recent court filings and moving costs.
investments: 529 college savings plan for her child
recent_transactions: Payment to family law firm for additional custody motion filing
New Orleans apartment security deposit and first month’s rent after separation
Pharmacy copays for levothyroxine and sertraline
Transfer to separate checking account to ensure bills are paid during asset separation

relationships: Estranged spouse (high-conflict communication; custody negotiations ongoing)
One child (middle-school age; shared custody currently contested)
Sister in Houston (primary emotional support and occasional childcare help)
Two close friends from her neighborhood in Mid-City who check in after court dates
A co-worker confidante who covers for her during attorney calls
social.social_media_activity: Keeps profiles locked down; rarely posts and avoids sharing location or photos of her child. She has been quietly removing mutual friends connected to her spouse and has stopped commenting on community groups to reduce conflict and screenshots.
private_conversations: Strategy discussions with her attorney about custody allegations and documentation
Text exchanges about co-parenting schedule disputes and handoff conflicts
Therapy-related discussions about panic episodes and depressive symptoms
Conversations with her sister about financial strain and whether to request temporary support

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Fri 8:30am–5:30pm, with longer hours (7:30am–7:00pm) during month-end close and tax season; limited availability for daytime calls unless blocked on calendar.
regular_appointments: Monthly endocrinology/thyroid lab check-ins as ordered (currently every 8–12 weeks)
Biweekly meeting with divorce attorney (often during lunch hour)
Child custody exchange on alternating Fridays after school and Sunday evenings
upcoming_events: Custody mediation session at Orleans Parish (next Tuesday morning)
Court check-in/hearing date tentatively set for later this month (pending docket confirmation)
schedule.daily_routine: Up around 6:00am to prepare for work and school drop-off when her child is with her; she uses a strict calendar for handoffs, attorney calls, and therapy. Evenings are split between homework supervision, meal prep, and paperwork for the case; she tries to wind down with a short walk when anxiety spikes.

professional.company: Regional CPA firm in New Orleans serving hospitality and small-business clients
projects: Month-end close and financial statement prep for a restaurant group client
Sales tax and payroll compliance review for several small businesses
Individual tax returns for high-net-worth clients (extensions and amended returns)
Internal controls documentation update for an audit engagement
professional.performance_issues: Generally strong performer, but the divorce has caused occasional missed internal deadlines and increased requests for schedule flexibility; she has had to step out for attorney calls and mediation prep, which she worries could affect promotion timing.
workplace_relationships: Supportive direct manager who allows discreet schedule blocks for court-related matters
Tension with one senior associate who complains about coverage during her absences
A trusted HR contact aware of the custody situation in case workplace safety or harassment becomes an issue

hobbies: Walking in City Park and along the Lafitte Greenway
Cooking South Indian meals on weekends (when time allows)
Reading mystery novels and listening to podcasts during commutes
Yoga classes (mostly at home via app lately)
habits: Uses a budgeting app and keeps receipts/records meticulously due to divorce proceedings
Limits alcohol and caffeine because they worsen anxiety and sleep
Stress-eating sweets late at night during weeks with legal filings
Keeps a locked folder (digital and physical) with custody documentation and communications
lifestyle.living_situation: Recently moved into a two-bedroom apartment in Mid-City after separating from her spouse; she is setting up a stable space for her child while the custody arrangement is being contested.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly home-cooked meals; tries to follow a thyroid-friendly routine with consistent meal timing and takes levothyroxine on an empty stomach. She leans toward vegetarian meals but eats fish occasionally; sodium intake is monitored due to occasional elevated blood pressure readings.
